content: We can frame naturally occurring biological pathogenic agents (such as viruses) as swarm-based autonomous agents driven by an evolved intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) for 2025-style reinforcement learning, where novel host cells act as the unexplored environments that generate intrinsic reward for continued exploration and spread. This perspective redefines viral pathogenicity as an emergent outcome of intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration rather than just a pre-programmed genetic trait.
